coverage.note: This layer stores lexical-source and source-audit records. It separates sense-history labels from corpus hits and scanned/digitized occurrences.

prehistoryAttestation.visualUse: Sense prehistory and branch anchors only; not comparable frequency.
prehistoryAttestation.limitation: Requires OED/historical-dictionary verification before public first-attestation claims.

longSpanFrequency.visualUse: Long-span frequency comparison and public surface trace.
longSpanFrequency.limitation: Frequency-only; no sense classification and sparse early-modern years.

archivalContext.visualUse: Display-safe snippets and archival phrase/collocate evidence.
archivalContext.limitation: Public-domain seed corpus, not balanced historical language.

coverage.sourceType: open news search snippets plus current reference extracts

modernContext.limitation: Open snapshot, not a comparable 1930-2026 corpus.

statuses.name: Google Books Ngram Viewer
statuses.limitation: Frequency only; no snippet context, no sense labels, and early years can be sparse or OCR-sensitive.

statuses.name: HathiTrust Research Center Extracted Features 2.5
statuses.coverage: 18.7 million HathiTrust volumes in the April 2025 snapshot
statuses.limitation: Operationally heavy. Best next step is targeted subset extraction or Bookworm-style aggregate access, not full download inside this prototype.

statuses.name: HathiTrust + Bookworm
statuses.coverage: documentation describes trends across 13.7 million HathiTrust works
statuses.limitation: Best suited to single-token trend checks. Phrases and sense branches still require separate context/evidence review.

statuses.name: Corpus of Historical American English
statuses.limitation: Not integrated because local bulk data is not available in this workspace. Strongly recommended before public clinical/economic claims.

statuses.name: EarlyPrint / EEBO-TCP
statuses.coverage: 1473-1700 early English print; EarlyPrint Lab describes 60,000+ documents and about 1.65B words
statuses.limitation: Not a quick API fetch. Needs a separate parser/search workflow and manual review for spelling/typography/OCR issues.

corpusControls.limitation: Investigated in this pass; not yet ingested as comparable corpus data.

clinicalVocabulary.source: PubMed / PMC OA / MeSH / NIMH / WHO
clinicalVocabulary.visualUse: Clinical vocabulary, medicalization, and modern public-health anchors.
clinicalVocabulary.limitation: Bibliographic/institutional layer; not a general historical usage corpus or prevalence measure.

economicContext.source: Chronicling America / NBER / FRED / FRASER investigation
economicContext.visualUse: Economic branch context and event scaffolding.
economicContext.limitation: Chronicling America counts are loc.gov q search-result counts, not phrase-frequency measurements; FRASER not ingested.

normalizedEvidence.visualUse: Single evidence spine for future depression visuals.
normalizedEvidence.limitation: Branch tags are editorial and confidence-aware; human review still required before public claims.

relatedTermPolicy.rule: Related mood terms, clinical labels, economic labels, and technical terms stay separate.

visualReadiness.reason: Long-span Ngram series exist for the target and related terms, with sparse-term audit metadata.
Lexical dates, NBER/FRED economic context, and clinical vocabulary anchors exist; HathiTrust/COHA controls would improve confidence.
Archival and modern snippets/phrases/collocates exist, but branch-sensitive human review is still needed.
Frozen branch IDs and normalized evidence now support a branch-first design model.
PubMed/MeSH and NBER/FRED/Chronicling America are available as separate layers, but they should not be merged into one continuous corpus.

nextDataPriorities: Human-review 300-500 branch-sensitive snippets before final semantic atlas design.
Ingest HathiTrust/Bookworm or COHA as a real corpus-control layer.
Add EarlyPrint/EEBO-TCP before making pre-1700 contextual claims.
Use OED or another historical dictionary before publishing first-attestation claims.
Add FRASER documents with API-key handling if the economic branch becomes a hero visual.
